questions.explanation: DDIC objects define reusable, centrally managed data definitions (tables, views, data elements, domains) used consistently across ABAP programs and the database.

options.A: To define and centrally manage data structures and their metadata for reuse across programs
options.B: To schedule background jobs and monitor their execution
options.C: To render UI controls in SAP Fiori applications
options.D: To encrypt database connections between application servers and SAP HANA

questions.question: In ABAP, what is the primary purpose of a Data Dictionary (DDIC) object such as a table or data element?

questions.explanation: Requirements analysis is where stakeholder needs are elicited, validated, and documented as functional and non-functional requirements.
questions.question: Which SDLC phase primarily focuses on validating requirements and defining what the system must do?

questions.explanation: WHERE filters individual rows prior to GROUP BY and aggregation; HAVING filters after aggregation.
questions.question: Which SQL clause is used to filter rows before aggregation occurs?

questions.explanation: SAP Gateway is the enablement layer that exposes business data via OData services and handles related runtime and management capabilities.

options.A: Expose and manage OData services for SAP and non-SAP consumers
options.B: Provide in-memory columnar storage for transactional and analytical workloads
options.C: Compile and deploy SAPUI5 applications to end-user devices
options.D: Act as the transport layer for ABAP change requests between systems

questions.question: In SAP architecture, what is the primary role of SAP Gateway?

questions.explanation: An NFR catalog with explicit acceptance criteria makes quality attributes measurable and testable, enabling architectural decisions and trade-offs.

options.A: A non-functional requirements (NFR) catalog with acceptance criteria
options.D: A list of developer IDE preferences

questions.question: When analyzing software specifications, which artifact best helps an architect identify measurable non-functional requirements (e.g., latency, availability, throughput)?

questions.explanation: Offline capability requires local storage (e.g., IndexedDB) plus sync logic (queues, retries, conflict resolution) to reconcile with the backend when connectivity returns.

options.A: Use a local persistence layer on the client and implement a synchronization mechanism with conflict handling
options.B: Rely solely on server-side rendering to avoid client state
options.C: Disable caching to ensure the latest data is always fetched
options.D: Use only synchronous OData calls and block the UI until completion

questions.question: You are creating a software design for an SAP Fiori app that must work offline and sync later. Which design approach best supports this requirement?

questions.explanation: In classic SAP architecture, ABAP AS runs application logic and uses the database (often HANA) for persistence and data-intensive operations.

options.A: The ABAP application server hosts business logic and communicates with the database for persistence and set-based processing
options.B: The database hosts the UI layer and communicates with ABAP for rendering
options.C: The ABAP application server is a replacement for the database and stores all data in memory only
options.D: The ABAP application server is only used for transport management and not for runtime execution

questions.question: In SAP NetWeaver-based landscapes, which statement best describes how the ABAP application server relates to the database (e.g., SAP HANA) from an architectural perspective?

questions.explanation: Architectural optimization for OData includes minimizing payloads ($select), controlling navigation ($expand), paging, and shaping data to UI needs (read model) to reduce round-trips and backend load.

options.A: Redesign the service to use server-side paging, $select/$expand appropriately, and introduce a purpose-built read model for the UI
options.B: Increase the UI refresh rate so the app feels more responsive
options.C: Move all filtering and joins to the client to reduce server load
options.D: Disable HTTPS to reduce encryption overhead

questions.question: A team exposes an OData service for a high-traffic SAPUI5 app. Performance degrades due to many round-trips and over-fetching. Which change is most architecturally appropriate?

questions.explanation: SAP BTP supports side-by-side extensibility, clean-core principles, and independent scaling while integrating via stable APIs/events rather than tight coupling or shared databases.

options.A: Build the extension on SAP BTP using loosely coupled services and integrate with ERP via published APIs/events
options.B: Implement all new logic as direct modifications in the ERP core to minimize network calls
options.C: Create a shared database schema between the extension and ERP to simplify data access
options.D: Deploy the UI only on end-user machines and store business data locally to avoid backend dependencies

questions.question: You must align a new extension app with the enterprise system architecture: core ERP must remain clean, integrations must be API-based, and the solution must scale independently. Which target architecture best fits?

questions.explanation: Git plus disciplined workflows (branching, PR reviews), automated CI/CD, and versioned artifacts provide traceability, repeatability, and controlled releases at scale.

options.A: Git with a defined branching strategy, pull requests with mandatory reviews, CI/CD pipelines, and versioned artifacts
options.B: Shared network folders with manual file naming conventions and ad-hoc email approvals
options.C: Local developer backups and informal verbal coordination for releases
options.D: A single long-lived branch with direct commits to production to reduce process overhead

questions.question: As an architect overseeing development, you need strong traceability from requirements to code changes and controlled releases across multiple teams. Which combination of configuration management tools/practices best supports this?
